LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS TERESA SU, ROBERT ROWEN'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER TRIAL PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1048(B) IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MOTION RAISES A TRIAL MANAGEMENT ISSUE THAT IS APPROPRIATELY DECIDED BY THE TRIAL JUDGE, NOT BY THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS STEPHEN MOSKOWITZ, MOSKOWITZ LAW CORPORATION, AND MOSKOWITZ LLP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT:DEFENDANTS STEPHEN MOSKOWITZ, MOSKOWITZ LAW CORPORATION AND MOSKOWITZ LLP'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST, SECOND, NINTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE COMPLAINT FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE, BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 17200 ET SEQ., AND VIOLATION OF THE CONSUMER LEGAL REMEDIES ACT IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312.JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Trial Ccp 1048(B)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney, David R. Socher of the law offices of David R. Socher (david@drsocher.com) appears as counsel for moving party, plaintiffs Teresa Su, et al.

Attorney, Alison Buchanan of the law offices of HOGE FENTON JONES & APPEL INC.(alison.buchanan@hogefenton.com) appears as counsel for opposing parties, defendants Stephen Moskowitz, et al.

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS TERESA SU, ROBERT ROWEN'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Trial per Code of Civil Procedure Section 1048(B) is DENIED without prejudice. The motion raises a trial management issue that is appropriately decided by the trial judge, not by the law and motion department.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MINI MINUTES: Defendant Stephen Moskowitz, Moskowitz Law Corporation, Moskowitz Llp Demurrer To Complaint (Tenative Rulings Only Part 2 Of 2)

duplicate entry only.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs STEPHEN MOSKOWITZ, MOSKOWITZ LAW CORPORATION, and MOSKOWITZ LLP's DEMURRER TO plaintiff's COMPLAINT:

Defendants Stephen Moskowitz, Moskowitz Law Corporation and Moskowitz LLP's demurrer to the first, second, ninth and eleventh causes of action of the complaint for professional negligence, breach of fiduciary duty, violation of Business and Professions Code 17200 et seq., and violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act is overruled. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for breach is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
